stories.name: kind-15-message-acceptance
stories.title: Accept and render NIP-17 kind-15 file messages
stories.description: Extend decryptNip17Message to accept kind-15 file messages (in addition to existing kind-14 support), build mediaJson from kind-15 tags, and render images inline. Kind-15 messages from other clients (e.g., using nostr.build) will display images instead of being silently dropped.

includes: crypto.ts: decryptNip17Message() accepts kind 14 AND kind 15
service.ts: ingestIncomingMessage() builds mediaJson from kind-15 tags (content→url, file-type→mimeType, dim→dimensions, blurhash→blurhash, x→sha256)
service.ts: processGiftWrapEvent() accepts kind 15 alongside kind 14
Kind-15 tag-to-mediaJson mapping: stores as {tags: [...]} format for consistency
Best-effort rendering for malformed kind-15 (missing file-type → fallback to URL extension MIME inference, missing dim/blurhash → defaults)
Thumb tag exclusion: always use content URL, ignore thumb tag if present
excludes: Inline URL detection in message text (Story 04 handles this)
Changes to outgoing message format (remains kind-14 only)

test_fixtures.description: Kind-15 wrapped events injected via relay for testing
test_fixtures.interface: NIP-17/59 gift-wrapped events with kind-15 rumor
test_fixtures.reason: Cannot programmatically send kind-15 from Nostling (outgoing is kind-14 only) - permanent test infrastructure

scenarios.name: kind-15-image-renders-inline
scenarios.description: Instance A injects kind-15 wrapped event via relay, Instance B receives and displays image inline in chat
user_steps: Setup: Create kind-15 wrapped event with image URL in content field and file-type tag
Instance A: Inject event into strfry relay via relay subscription or debug tool
Instance B: Navigate to chat with Instance A
Instance B: Wait for message to appear in conversation
Instance B: Verify image is visible inline (not silently dropped)
assertions: Image element visible in Instance B chat
Screenshot shows rendered image (not URL text)
Database query: media_json column contains parsed kind-15 tags

scenarios.name: kind-15-malformed-tags-fallback
scenarios.description: Kind-15 message with missing file-type tag renders image via MIME inference from URL extension
user_steps: Setup: Create kind-15 wrapped event WITHOUT file-type tag but with .jpg URL extension
Instance A: Inject event into relay
Instance B: Navigate to chat, wait for message
Instance B: Verify image renders (MIME inferred from extension)
assertions: Image renders despite missing file-type tag

scenarios.name: kind-15-thumb-tag-ignored
scenarios.description: Kind-15 message with thumb tag ignores thumbnail URL and renders full-size image
user_steps: Setup: Create kind-15 with both content URL and thumb tag
Instance A: Inject event
Instance B: Navigate to chat, wait for message
Instance B: Verify full-size image URL is loaded (not thumb URL)
assertions: Image src attribute matches content URL (not thumb URL)
Database: media_json contains only content URL

stories.notes: Foundation story enabling kind-15 support. Uses test fixtures for injecting kind-15 events since Nostling only sends kind-14. Depends on Story 02 for MIME inference when file-type tag is missing.

stories.name: mime-type-inference
stories.title: Infer MIME type from URL extension and Content-Type header
stories.description: When incoming media attachment lacks explicit MIME type (missing 'm' field in imeta or 'file-type' in kind-15), infer MIME from URL file extension. Use Content-Type header from blossom server as secondary signal. Enables rendering of attachments from clients that omit MIME metadata.

includes: media-parser.ts: parseImetaTags() infers MIME from URL extension when m field missing

Extension-to-MIME mapping: .jpg/.jpeg→image/jpeg, .png→image/png, .gif→image/gif, .webp→image/webp, .avif→image/avif, .bmp→image/bmp, .svg→image/svg+xml, .tiff/.tif→image/tiff, .heic/.heif→image/heic, .mp4→video/mp4
CachedImage: Use Content-Type header from blossom server response as MIME type signal when available
Blossom blob URLs without extension: Default to attempting image rendering, show placeholder if Content-Type indicates non-image
MIME inference is pure, synchronous operation (no network requests at parse time)
Tag value priority: Trust explicit tag MIME over URL extension when both present
excludes: Extended format rendering logic (Story 03 handles isRenderableImageMimeType)
Content-Type sniffing for application/octet-stream (security constraint - never render)

test_fixtures.description: Mock blossom server returning various Content-Type headers
test_fixtures.interface: HTTP server serving /blob/<hash> with configurable Content-Type headers
test_fixtures.reason: Cannot control real blossom server Content-Type responses - permanent test infrastructure

stories.notes: Independent story handling MIME inference. Can be tested standalone with imeta-only messages (no kind-15 dependency). Uses test fixtures for controlling blossom server Content-Type responses.

stories.name: extended-image-formats
stories.title: Support extended image formats for incoming messages
stories.description: Recognize and render additional image MIME types for incoming messages (AVIF, BMP, SVG, HEIC, TIFF). Chromium-supported formats render inline; unsupported formats (HEIC, TIFF) show download link. Does NOT change outgoing attachment validation (remains JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP).

includes: media-parser.ts: isImageMimeType() recognizes image/avif, image/bmp, image/svg+xml, image/heic, image/heif, image/tiff
media-parser.ts: NEW isRenderableImageMimeType() function distinguishes Chromium-native formats from non-renderable
Renderable formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, AVIF, BMP, SVG (Chromium native support)
Non-renderable formats: HEIC, HEIF, TIFF (show download link instead)
MediaAttachments.tsx: Use isRenderableImageMimeType() to decide inline render vs download link
SVG security: Render via tag (script-safe), never via
HTTPS-only image URL rendering: Block http://, javascript:, data:, file:// URLs
excludes: Changes to attachment-validation.ts (outgoing validation remains unchanged)

test_fixtures.description: Test image files in various formats (AVIF, BMP, SVG, HEIC, TIFF) hosted on blossom server
test_fixtures.reason: Need controlled test images for format verification - permanent test infrastructure

stories.notes: Independent story focused on format support. Can be tested standalone with kind-14 imeta messages. Uses test fixtures for various image format files. Security-critical: SVG script safety verification mandatory.

stories.name: inline-url-detection
stories.title: Detect and render image URLs inline in message text
stories.description: When message text content contains image URLs (without structured imeta/kind-15 attachments), detect and render them as inline images instead of text links. Enables rendering of images from clients that send URLs in content field without metadata tags.

includes: linkify.ts: detectImageUrls() function identifies image URLs via extension (.jpg, .jpeg, .png, .gif, .webp, .avif, .bmp, .svg) or blossom URL pattern (/blob/ in path)
main.tsx: MessageContent component renders detected image URLs as CachedImage components, replacing URL text
HTTPS-only URL filtering: Only render https:// URLs, block http://, javascript:, data:, file://
No double-rendering: Skip inline detection when mediaJson is present (structured attachments take precedence)
Lazy loading: Images below viewport load on scroll (rely on browser native lazy loading or IntersectionObserver)
Failed image loads: Display placeholder/broken image indicator via CachedImage fallback
No limit on inline images per message (lazy loading handles performance)
Lightbox click-to-expand interaction (consistent with existing ImageAttachment behavior)
excludes: Changes to existing MediaAttachments component (inline images are separate render path)

stories.notes: Core inline rendering feature. Independent of kind-15 support (works with kind-14 messages). Reuses existing CachedImage component for rendering. Security-critical: HTTPS-only URL filtering mandatory.

stories.name: unknown-kind-handling
stories.title: Graceful handling of unknown wrapped event kinds
stories.description: When receiving wrapped events of unrecognized kinds (not kind 14 or 15), log them at info level and discard gracefully without user-visible errors. Prevents silent failures and improves debuggability while maintaining forward compatibility with future protocol extensions.

includes: service.ts: processGiftWrapEvent() handles kind 14 and 15, logs other kinds at info level
Info-level logging for unrecognized kinds: include kind number and event ID
Graceful discard: no database insertion, no error toast, no UI glitch
Never show error to user for unrecognized kinds (sender may use newer protocol version)
Existing behavior for kind 14 and 15 unchanged (additive change only)
excludes: Changes to error handling for malformed events (out of scope)

test_fixtures.description: Wrapped events with unrecognized kinds (e.g., kind 42 for community posts) injected via relay
test_fixtures.interface: NIP-17/59 gift-wrapped events with various kind numbers
test_fixtures.reason: Cannot send arbitrary kinds from Nostling - permanent test infrastructure

Instance B: Navigate to chat with Instance A
Instance B: Verify no error toast appears
Instance B: Verify info-level log entry with kind number and event ID
assertions: Info log entry: 'Received unrecognized wrapped event kind: 42'
Database: No message inserted for kind-42 event

stories.notes: Small independent story improving error handling. Can be tested standalone with injected events. Uses test fixtures for unrecognized kind events.

stories.name: regression-verification
stories.title: E2E regression verification for existing kind-14 messages
stories.description: Verify that all changes to kind-15 support, MIME inference, and inline URL detection do NOT break existing kind-14 message rendering (text-only, imeta attachments). Verify outgoing message format unchanged. Comprehensive regression suite ensuring backward compatibility.

includes: E2E verification: Kind-14 text-only messages render identically to pre-implementation baseline
E2E verification: Kind-14 with imeta tags render identically to pre-implementation baseline
E2E verification: Outgoing messages still send kind-14 with imeta tags (kind-15 is receive-only)
Unit test: parseMediaJson() handles both {attachments: [...]} and {tags: [...]} formats without breaking
Visual regression: Screenshot comparison for existing message types
Database verification: Existing messages in database render correctly after upgrade

scenarios.name: mediajson-backward-compatibility
scenarios.description: parseMediaJson() correctly parses both old and new format messages from database
user_steps: Setup: Seed database with messages in both {attachments: [...]} and {tags: [...]} formats
Instance B: Navigate to conversation containing both formats

assertions: Screenshots show correct image rendering for both formats

stories.notes: Regression verification story ensuring backward compatibility. Should be executed AFTER all other stories are implemented. Uses baseline screenshots for visual regression testing. No new features, only verification.

intermediaries.artifact: ingestIncomingMessage builds mediaJson from kind-15 tags

seams.data: MIME type when missing from tags

dependency_notes: Stories are designed to be independent and can be implemented in any order. Story 02 (MIME inference) and Story 03 (extended formats) are pure utility functions that enhance Story 01 (kind-15) and Story 04 (inline URLs), but are NOT dependencies - they fail gracefully without each other. Story 06 (regression verification) should be executed last as it validates all previous stories together. Recommended order: 02, 03, 01, 04, 05, 06 (utilities first, then features, then regression).
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks introduced. All mock_dependencies and introduces_mocks arrays are empty. Test fixtures used where needed (kind-15 event injection, blossom server responses, test images) are permanent testing infrastructure, not temporary mocks requiring resolution.
